experiment_section: Experiment 1: Intrinsic Information Preferences and Skewness

instructions.description: General study instructions: rules of conduct, overview of Study 1 (pen vs postcard) and Study 2 (lottery), payment info, and random number generator explanation
Practice round (A1): elicitation of preference between pen and postcard — practice for the main choice task
Practice round (A2): elicitation of preference strength for pen vs postcard — practice for preference intensity rating
Willingness to Switch Elicitation: transcription of video instructions explaining the 10-question switching task and incentive mechanism
Practice round (A3): willingness to switch elicitation (10 questions, 1–50 cents compensation) for pen vs postcard — practice for the main switching task
Overview of checks and ancillary questions: comprehension questions, attention checks, confusion prompt, demographics, happiness questionnaire, and filler task instructions
Screenshot A6 — Preamble to willingness to switch elicitation: displays chosen option graphically and explains the switching mechanism and random question selection

screens.description: Screenshot A4 — Choice page for T3: participant chooses between Option 1 (positively skewed: 20% red/80% black overall; red→75% win, black→44% win) and Option 2 (negatively skewed: 80% red/20% black overall; red→56% win, black→25% win)
Screenshot A5 — Preference strength elicitation: 0–10 scale rating how strongly participant preferred their chosen option over the alternative

parameters.scale_min_label: Completely indifferent between the two options
parameters.scale_max_label: Very strong preference for chosen option

screens.description: Screenshot A7 — Willingness to switch elicitation: 10 questions asking if participant would change their information structure choice for monetary compensation ranging from 1 to 50 cents
